calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Advanced From Jan-07-20 To Dec-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Caroline Hinshaw for Wendy Gordy (Beneficiary) via Court Call.
Melanie Emmons (in person) and Rowena Navia (via Court Call) for Interested Party Kato Feder & Suzuki LLP as former attorney for Wendy Gordy.
Jennifer McGuire for Interested Party John Balquist via Court Call.
Maria Lawless for Celeste Kremer (Beneficiary).

The Court inquired as to notice issues to Leslie Sanford and Wendy Gordy. Ms. McGuire stated that Leslie Sanford was noticed out of courtesy, that it was not needed, and she is not a beneficiary. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Gordy did receive notice and that per notice statute she can sign for her client. The Court noted that moving forward the client should sign waivers herself.

The Court raised issue on request for extraordinary fees from the McGuire firm with 850 petition and Kato Feder firm with sale of property and locating assets. Ms. McGuire stated there was no executed copy for trust found, liquid assets were held in accounts in name of trust, house was not in name of trust and the court did not approve bringing the house into trust. Ms. Hinshaw stated her client does not object. Ms. Lawless stated her client does not object, that the circumstances of it being a hoarder house warrants extraordinary fees, and her firm waived any extraordinary fees. Ms. Emmons stated Kato Feder spend 13.8 hours going through property to sell and 13.6 hours for locating assets. Ms. Navia stated there were hazardous material to filter through, time to organize the items to be distributed, locating paperwork, jewelry, fur and gold coins and they didn't discount anything the clients said were supposed to be in the house. Ms. McGuire stated the original petition for probate was filed by Joyce Anderson and there was concern if Joyce entered the house to steal items.

Ms. Lawless and Ms. Hinshaw presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Ms. Gordy's costs requests.

The Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Naznin Challa for Celeste Gordy.
Wendy Gordy appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Heather Hoekstra for and with John Balquist (Special Administrator).

Ms. Challa stated that the amended petition was a joint petition and that she stands by their amended petition to appoint Mr. Balquist. Ms. Gordy expressed concern about Celeste's criminal record and health issues, requested someone other than Mr. Balquist to serve, stated that she felt harassed into signing the papers, asked for more time to come up with someone else that they could agree on and hire another Attorney, and stated that she wants someone that she can trust and will remain neutral. The Court noted that this continuance was exactly for that to happen. Ms. Gordy also stated that she made several attempts to communicate with Celeste and Maria, that they won't communicate with her, that she suggested mediation and they refused to participate, that she wants things to be fair and honest, that she wants this to finish and that she does not want to be the sole Administrator. Ms. Challa stated that they have been down this path before, expressed concern about Ms. Gordy changing her mind again, stated that she just wants an Administrator appointed at this point and that she wants to move forward with Mr. Balquist. Ms. Challa also stated that the property is not titled in the Decedent's name, that it is in trust, that they will need to file a petition to put it in the estate and that they could not find a trust document. Ms. Gordy stated that if Mr. Balquist would agree to communicate with her, she would be okay with him serving. The Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission. The Court stated that it will either grant the petition to appoint Mr. Balquist or restore it back to calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Continued To Dec-15-17 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for Wendy Gordy (Petitioner).
Maria Lawless for Celeste Kremer.
Nathan Pastor for and with John Balquist.

Mr. Wrobel stated that he submitted a petition yesterday. The Court expressed that this matter calls out for a Professional Fiduciary, that Ms. Gordy's position is troubling and suggests that the parties agree on a proposed Professional. Ms. Lawless stated that she agrees the matter will benefit from a Professional, that the letters of special administration expired almost a month ago and raised the issue of bond. The Court noted that the issue raised of documents in possession of Ms. Navia and who will be retrieving said documents is not before the Court, and stated that it hopes the parties can cooperate to avoid battling. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to January 31, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
